"Localhost Refused to Connect" on Taiwan's HiCOS Citizen Certificate? Here's the Fix
Localhost refused to connect on Taiwan's HiCOS Citizen Certificate
When the HiCOS test page shows "localhost refused to connect", it's almost always because
I've been working with some Taiwan government websites lately, which means using the Citizen Digital Certificate. For some reason it just wouldn't work, so I'm writing this down for next time.
If you're using a Taiwan government site and you don't have
installed yet, you'll be prompted to install it from
HiCOS is Taiwan's official Citizen Digital Certificate verification package. Without it, you can't use the certificate at all.
Here's where it gets interesting. After installing HiCOS, the test page at
keeps showing "localhost refused to connect" (each browser phrases it differently):
You might try the "HiCOS Client System Environment Test Tool", and find that everything checks out:
HiCOS Client System Environment Test Tool
But the browser still refuses to connect. Here's how to fix it.
isn't running in the background. So we just need to restart it.
Double-click it. A few windows should pop up — once you see this status window, ChkSrv is up:
Now reload the browser and go to
That should be enough. If it still doesn't work, reboot the computer and start ChkSrv.exe again — it usually clears up after that.

ChkSrv is supposed to auto-start after a reboot. If it doesn't, it's usually because something on your system is blocking it — could be a startup-item manager, could be antivirus software. Whitelist ChkSrv.exe, or temporarily disable your antivirus and try again. The way ChkSrv works is by running a local server on your machine — the Citizen Digital Certificate uses that server for verification.
A few useful ChkSrv URLs for reference:
ChkSrv's home page — just shows the HiPKI version, e.g.
The test page for verifying the Citizen Digital Certificate is working
The popup that appears when you actually use the certificate — your browser needs to allow this URL, otherwise the certificate flow won't work
Honestly, HiCOS's UX is rough. When it breaks, there's basically no guidance on what to look at, which is brutal for regular users.

If you're trying to use Taiwan's Citizen Digital Certificate on a government site and your browser keeps showing "localhost refused to connect" — usually the problem is ChkSrv.exe not running in the background. This post walks through how to fix it.
